title: Execution Model
text:
The library MUST run its event loop on a dedicated OS thread spawned at Agent construction time. The library MUST NOT require an async runtime.

The event loop MUST poll the UDP socket for incoming datagrams and drive the DTLS state machine in response to socket readiness and timer events.

The event loop MUST drain a command channel between socket polls to process application requests (MIB updates, trap sends, certificate replacements).

The library MUST NOT perform blocking operations that could stall the event loop. Any long-running work MUST be kept off the event loop thread.

**Rationale:**
A dedicated OS thread with a synchronous event loop is the simplest execution model for driving the dimpl sans-IO DTLS engine. It avoids the complexity of an async runtime for a low-throughput SNMP workload. Owning all state on a single thread eliminates the need for locking. A command channel provides the boundary between application threads and the event loop.
